error: The sandbox was not found: This error is likely due to sandbox timeout. You can modify the sandbox timeout by passing 'timeout' when starting the sandbox or calling '.set_timeout' on the sandbox with the desired timeout.

validate.status: partial - core tests pass, rubric broken
validate.notes: Docker build, NOP (reward=0), and Gold (reward=1) all pass. Rubric judge returns 0.0 because /workspace/repo/ does not exist on host - the Payload git repo is only inside the Docker container at /workspace/payload, not accessible to the judge on the host filesystem. The rubric rules were also restructured to have proper source dicts and references, but the judge cannot function without a git repo to diff against. This is a fundamental setup incompatibility: rubric judge expects repo on host, but task runs entirely inside Docker.
